This number was estimated in the past and in 2018 the actual distance traveled by equipment was collected and reported on. comments.comment:
Decrease in percent fuel oil in ANFO used in 2023 - The emission rate calculation for NOx considers a multiplier based on the percent fuel oil in ANFO.
2022 – 6% fuel oil, NOx EF multiplier = 1
2023 – 4% fuel oil, NOx EF multiplier = 2.2

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The Mining Association of Canada conducted a comparative review of reported NPRI emissions from various mining facilities across Canada.  This review indicated that the reported particulate emissions for Teck Coal were high relative to other operations.  Teck Coal performed a detailed review of their emission calculations to identify areas of conservatism with the intention of refining the calculated values to reflect releases more accurately.  As a result of this review, the following changes were made, which resulted in significant decreases in the quantity of fugitive dust reported for the 2023 reporting year.  This is understood to represent a more accurate picture of emissions at Teck Coal operations.

Previous estimates for fugitive dust were calculated using site specific values for silt and moisture content for coal and waste rock.  As a result of the detailed review conducted for the 2023 reporting year, it was determined that the site-specific silt content does not represent the proper size fraction range for use with U.S. EPA AP-42 calculations.  Therefore, the U.S. EPA AP-42 typical mean values for silt and moisture content for western surface coal mining were used for coal and waste rock.

Additionally, previous estimates for fugitive dust from stockpiles, dumps, and other exposed areas conservatively assumed that the entire area was active, with activity from bulldozers and other equipment as well as wind erosion.  In order to reduce conservatisms and represent a more accurate operational scenario for the 2023 reporting year, it was estimated that 20% of spoil areas were active and 75% of all other area types were active.  The inactive areas were assumed to only be impacted by wind erosion.

Lastly, calculations for fugitive dust released from blasting were updated.  Previously, the entire horizontal area blasted in the reporting year was considered.  For the 2023 reporting year, the calculation was updated to consider the average horizontal blast area per blast and the number of blasts in 2023.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

As a result of this review, the following changes were made, which resulted in significant decreases in the quantity of fugitive dust reported for the 2023 reporting year.  This is understood to represent a more accurate picture of emissions at Teck Coal operations.

Previous estimates for fugitive dust were calculated using site specific values for silt and moisture content for coal and waste rock.  As a result of the detailed review conducted for the 2023 reporting year, it was determined that the site-specific silt content does not represent the proper size fraction range for use with U.S. EPA AP-42 calculations.  Therefore, the U.S. EPA AP-42 typical mean values for silt and moisture content for western surface coal mining were used for coal and waste rock.

Additionally, previous estimates for fugitive dust from stockpiles, dumps, and other exposed areas conservatively assumed that the entire area was active, with activity from bulldozers and other equipment as well as wind erosion.  In order to reduce conservatisms and represent a more accurate operational scenario for the 2023 reporting year, it was estimated that 20% of spoil areas were active and 75% of all other area types were active.  The inactive areas were assumed to only be impacted by wind erosion.

Lastly, calculations for fugitive dust released from blasting were updated.  Previously, the entire horizontal area blasted in the reporting year was considered.  For the 2023 reporting year, the calculation was updated to consider the average horizontal blast area per blast and the number of blasts in 2023.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The Mining Association of Canada conducted a comparative review of reported NPRI emissions from various mining facilities across Canada.  This review indicated that the reported particulate emissions for Teck Coal were high relative to other operations.  Teck Coal performed a detailed review of their emission calculations to identify areas of conservatism with the intention of refining the calculated values to reflect releases more accurately.  As a result of this review, the following changes were made, which resulted in significant decreases in the quantity of fugitive dust reported for the 2023 reporting year.  This is understood to represent a more accurate picture of emissions at Teck Coal operations.

Previous estimates for fugitive dust were calculated using site specific values for silt and moisture content for coal and waste rock.  As a result of the detailed review conducted for the 2023 reporting year, it was determined that the site-specific silt content does not represent the proper size fraction range for use with U.S. EPA AP-42 calculations.  Therefore, the U.S. EPA AP-42 typical mean values for silt and moisture content for western surface coal mining were used for coal and waste rock.

Additionally, previous estimates for fugitive dust from stockpiles, dumps, and other exposed areas conservatively assumed that the entire area was active, with activity from bulldozers and other equipment as well as wind erosion.  In order to reduce conservatisms and represent a more accurate operational scenario for the 2023 reporting year, it was estimated that 20% of spoil areas were active and 75% of all other area types were active.  The inactive areas were assumed to only be impacted by wind erosion.

Lastly, calculations for fugitive dust released from blasting were updated.  Previously, the entire horizontal area blasted in the reporting year was considered.  For the 2023 reporting year, the calculation was updated to consider the average horizontal blast area per blast and the number of blasts in 2023.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Sampling locations were adjusted between 2023 and 2024 to include seven additional discharge sources. The contribution from Greenhills Operations that used to be attributed to Fording River Operations was also included in the loads. 
Improved use of continuous flow and water quality coverage in 2024.
